summary: Russian forces launched widespread drone attacks across multiple Ukrainian regions including Kyiv, Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Poltavska, Sumska, and Odesa oblasts, with air raid alerts declared in Kyiv. The Russian Defense Ministry reported liberation of Zapselie and Riasnoe in Sumy region, while a Russian Iskander missile struck a UN World Food Programme warehouse in Dnipro destroying aid worth $1.4 million. Russia recommended foreigners leave Kyiv and issued threats of strikes on the capital, while Ukraine's SBS commander warned Belarus's Lukashenko that 500 targets on Belarusian territory had already been identified.
key_points: Multiple UAV groups detected from Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Kursk, and Black Sea directions heading toward Kyiv, Poltava, Dnipro, and other regions (Source: @kpszsu/62885)
Russian Iskander missile struck UN World Food Programme warehouse in Dnipro, destroying food aid sufficient for 130,000 people valued at $1.4 million (Source: @UkraineNow/81473)
Russian Defense Ministry announced liberation of Zapselie and Riasnoe in Sumy region after weeks of fighting (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30438)
Russia recommended foreigners evacuate Kyiv and threatened strikes on military and administrative infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/377659)
Ukraine's SBS commander Magyar warned Belarus's Lukashenko that first 500 targets on Belarusian territory have already been identified (Source: @UkraineNow/81475)
Ukraine's Interior Minister stated Ukraine loses 70-100 civilians weekly due to Russian drone, ballistic missile, cruise missile, and aerial bomb attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81472)
Russian strategic aviation command posts active in Far East and western Russia with Tu-95MS aircraft likely relocating to airfields closer to Ukraine (Source: @eRadarrua/93762)
KABs reported targeting Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Donetsk oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/62865)

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces conducted a large-scale multi-axis drone attack across northern, eastern, and southern Ukraine with air raid alerts in Kyiv, while simultaneously a Russian Iskander missile destroyed a UN World Food Programme warehouse in Dnipro containing aid for 130,000 people. The Russian Defense Ministry announced the liberation of two settlements in Sumy region, and Russia issued formal recommendations for foreigners to evacuate Kyiv while threatening strikes on military and administrative infrastructure. Ukraine's leadership escalated rhetoric against Belarus, with the SBS commander identifying 500 pre-designated targets on Belarusian territory should Lukashenko enter the war on Russia's side.

points: Ukraine's Interior Minister Ihor Klymenko stated that Ukraine loses between 70 and 100 civilians every week as a result of Russian attacks using drones, ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, and aerial bombs, underscoring the sustained toll of Russian long-range strikes on civilian populations (Source: @UkraineNow/81472)

A Russian Iskander missile conducted a targeted strike on a UN World Food Programme warehouse in Dnipro, destroying a significant quantity of food aid intended for populations near the front line; the destroyed supplies were sufficient to support 130,000 people and had an estimated value of $1.4 million (Source: @UkraineNow/81473)

Ukraine's SBS (Special Forces) Commander Magyar issued a direct warning to Belarusian leader Lukashenko, stating that the first 500 targets on Belarusian territory had already been identified and advising against Belarusian participation in the war on Russia's side (Source: @UkraineNow/81475)

The SBU detained a mayor of a city in Odesa Oblast who demanded a $35,000 bribe from a foreign-invested company for permitting construction of a wind power plant intended to serve as an alternative energy source for hospitals, schools, and kindergartens; the official faces up to 12 years imprisonment with property confiscation (Source: @UkraineNow/81470)

Ukraine's Prosecutor General revealed that TCC (military commissariat) employees falsely mobilized 276 deceased individuals and prisoners in order to report successful fulfillment of mobilization plans, exposing systemic fraud within the conscription system (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30436)

Former Ukrainian Foreign Minister Kuleba assessed that Putin is sending a signal to Trump by threatening strikes on Kiev, interpreting the threats as an invitation for Trump to pressure Ukraine into capitulation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30439)

The EU confirmed it will not evacuate diplomats from Kiev despite Russia's warning, with EC spokesperson Anita Hipper stating the decision stands, while Slovakia's PM Fico stated many EU countries are ready to join a complaint against the European Commission over the ban on Russian gas imports (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30447)

The Russian Defense Ministry announced that the 'North' military group liberated the populated areas of Zapselie and Riasnoe in Sumy region, with battles for both villages lasting several weeks before Russian forces secured control (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30438)

Russia's Ministry of Transport and Rosaviatsiya received a special flight regime notification from the Russian Defense Ministry for the Moscow air zone, affecting small aviation operations while regular and charter passenger flights remain unaffected (Source: @tass_agency/377662)

Russia recommended that foreigners leave Kyiv as soon as possible and advised Kyiv residents to avoid military and administrative infrastructure objects, signaling potential escalation of strikes on the Ukrainian capital (Source: @tass_agency/377659)

Russian Ambassador to Germany expressed disappointment that the German government found no words of sympathy regarding the tragedy in Starobilsk, with Russia's UN envoy Nebenzya expressing skepticism about international investigations into the Ukrainian strike on a pedagogical college there (Source: @tass_agency/377651)

Russian strategic aviation command posts showed increased activity in the Far East and western Russia, with Tu-95MS aircraft detected in flight likely conducting redeployment to airfields closer to Ukraine in preparation for potential long-range strike operations (Source: @eRadarrua/93762)

Despite a Moscow arbitration court ruling, Euroclear Bank stated that Russian Central Bank assets will remain frozen, citing that the claim is not recognized under EU legislation and that Euroclear does not recognize the jurisdiction of Russian courts (Source: @tass_agency/377657)

Russian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Maria Zakharova criticized UN Secretary-General Guterres for expressing concern over Russian strike plans on Kiev while remaining silent about what Russia characterizes as Ukrainian killings of children in Starobilsk (Source: @tass_agency/377668)

Russian forces reported liberation of Zapselie and Riasnoe in Sumy region after weeks of sustained fighting for both villages, representing incremental Russian advances in the northeastern operational zone (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30438)

Multiple UAV groups were detected from Kharkiv Oblast heading toward Poltavska Oblast with vectors targeting Chutovoe, and from Kharkiv toward Bohodukhiv and Kharkiv airport, indicating continued Russian drone pressure on eastern Ukrainian logistics and population centers (Source: @kpszsu/62884)

A group of UAVs was detected on the border of Poltavska and Kharkiv oblasts heading in the direction of Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, suggesting Russian attempts to extend drone strikes deeper into eastern Ukrainian rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/62887)

KABs (guided aerial bombs) were reported targeting Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Donetsk oblasts, indicating continued Russian use of air-delivered precision munitions against eastern front positions and infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/62865)
A new group of UAVs was detected on the northwestern part of Kharkiv Oblast heading southward, adding to the multi-directional drone threat against the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/62887)

Multiple reactive (jet-powered) UAVs were detected in Chernihiv Oblast heading toward Snovsk, Ripky, and Horodnia, with additional UAVs detected in Bryansk Oblast (Russia) near Klimovo expected to enter Chernihiv Oblast shortly (Source: @eRadarrua/93740)

A reactive UAV was detected in Chernihiv Oblast heading toward northern Kyiv Oblast, and another reactive UAV was tracked near Klimovo in Bryansk Oblast, indicating a concentrated northern drone axis toward the Ukrainian capital (Source: @kpszsu/62885)

UAVs were detected in the Chernobyl Exclusion Zone heading toward Zhytomyr Oblast, with additional reactive UAVs passing through Khrasytychi toward Zhytomyr Oblast and another reactive UAV tracked toward Slavutych (Source: @eRadarrua/93799)

An air raid alert was declared in Kyiv at 17:42 local time on May 26, 2026, with the alert lifted at 18:06, confirming the operational reality of the northern drone threat to the capital (Source: @eRadarrua/93769)

A group of UAVs from Kursk Oblast (Russia) was detected heading toward Sumy Oblast, and UAVs from southern Sumy Oblast were heading toward Poltavska Oblast and Chernihiv Oblast, indicating multi-axis northern pressure (Source: @kpszsu/62862)
A reactive UAV was detected heading toward Narodychi in Zhytomyr Oblast, and a group of UAVs was detected in the center of Sumy Oblast heading toward Konotop and Krolevets (Source: @kpszsu/62887)

A hostile Su-30 aircraft was operating in Belarusian airspace near Kobryn (opposite Volyn Oblast), indicating potential Belarusian airspace being used for Russian aerial operations against northwestern Ukraine (Source: @eRadarrua/93778)

UAVs from the Black Sea were detected heading toward Odesa Oblast with a vector targeting Serhiivka, indicating continued Russian use of maritime-launched drone attacks against southern Ukrainian coastal areas (Source: @kpszsu/62882)

UAVs from Kherson Oblast were detected heading toward Kryvyi Rih in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, and additional UAVs on the western part of Dnipropetrovsk Oblast were also heading toward Kryvyi Rih, suggesting concentrated strikes on this key southern city (Source: @kpszsu/62875)
In Mykolaiv Oblast, a UAV was detected in the southern part of the oblast heading in a northwesterly direction, adding to the multi-axis southern drone threat (Source: @kpszsu/62899)

UAVs on the border of Poltavska and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts were heading in a west/southwesterly direction, and UAVs in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast were heading toward Zhovti Vody/Piatykhatky, indicating continued pressure on southern and southeastern Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/62884)
Three UAVs from Dnipropetrovsk Oblast (from Sofiivka) were heading toward Zhovti Vody, and a UAV was detected heading toward Dnipro city from the northeast (Source: @eRadarrua/93785)

A large-scale multi-axis drone attack was conducted across Ukraine with UAVs detected from Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Kursk, Bryansk, Black Sea, Kherson, and Belarusian directions, targeting Kyiv, Poltava, Dnipro, Zhytomyr, Odesa, and other regions simultaneously (Source: @kpszsu/62885)

Reactive (jet-powered) UAVs were deployed from multiple directions including Chernihiv Oblast toward Snovsk, Ripky, Horodnia, northern Kyiv Oblast, and Zhytomyr Oblast, representing a higher-speed and potentially harder-to-intercept threat compared to conventional propeller drones (Source: @eRadarrua/93740)

Russian strategic aviation command posts in the Far East and western Russia showed heightened activity with Tu-95MS aircraft detected in flight, likely conducting redeployment to airfields closer to Ukraine in preparation for potential long-range cruise missile strikes (Source: @eRadarrua/93762)

A Russian Iskander ballistic missile was used to strike the UN World Food Programme warehouse in Dnipro, demonstrating continued employment of precision ballistic missiles against high-value targets (Source: @UkraineNow/81473)

KABs (guided aerial bombs) were deployed against Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Donetsk oblasts, indicating sustained Russian use of air-delivered precision munitions requiring aircraft to operate at or near the front line (Source: @kpszsu/62865)

A hostile Su-30 aircraft was detected operating in Belarusian airspace near Kobryn opposite Volyn Oblast, suggesting potential use of Belarusian territory for Russian fighter operations against northwestern Ukraine (Source: @eRadarrua/93778)

Three camera-equipped controlled UAVs were detected in the Chutovoe area of Poltavska Oblast, suggesting reconnaissance-strike drone operations with real-time visual targeting capability (Source: @eRadarrua/93791)

key_developments: Russian forces conducted a large-scale coordinated drone attack across multiple axes with UAVs from Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Kursk, Bryansk, Black Sea, and Kherson directions targeting Kyiv, Poltava, Dnipro, Zhytomyr, and Odesa regions, with air raid alerts declared in the capital (Source: @kpszsu/62885)

A Russian Iskander missile struck a UN World Food Programme warehouse in Dnipro, destroying food aid sufficient for 130,000 people valued at approximately $1.4 million, in what was described as a targeted attack on humanitarian infrastructure near the front line (Source: @UkraineNow/81473)

The Russian Defense Ministry announced liberation of Zapselie and Riasnoe in Sumy region by the 'North' military group after weeks of sustained fighting for both settlements, marking incremental Russian territorial gains in the northeastern sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30438)

Russia formally recommended that foreigners evacuate Kyiv and warned residents to avoid military and administrative infrastructure, while former FM Kuleba assessed Putin is signaling Trump to pressure Ukraine into capitulation (Source: @tass_agency/377659)

Ukraine's SBS Commander Magyar issued a direct threat to Belarusian leader Lukashenko stating that the first 500 targets on Belarusian territory had been pre-identified should Belarus enter the war on Russia's side (Source: @UkraineNow/81475)

Russian Tu-95MS strategic bombers were detected relocating from the Far East and western Russia to airfields closer to Ukraine, with heightened strategic aviation command post activity suggesting preparation for potential long-range cruise missile strikes (Source: @eRadarrua/93762)

Ukraine's Interior Minister reported weekly civilian casualties of 70-100 from Russian drone, ballistic missile, cruise missile, and aerial bomb attacks, while the Prosecutor General exposed TCC fraud involving 276 falsely mobilized deceased and imprisoned individuals (Source: @UkraineNow/81472)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational picture indicates a significant escalation in Russian long-range strike operations, with a large-scale multi-axis drone attack employing both conventional and reactive (jet-powered) UAVs across northern, eastern, and southern Ukraine simultaneously. The redeployment of Tu-95MS strategic bombers closer to Ukraine, combined with Russia's formal recommendation for foreigners to evacuate Kyiv and threats against military-administrative infrastructure, suggests preparation for a potential major strike campaign against the Ukrainian capital and strategic rear areas. The destruction of the UN WFP warehouse in Dnipro with an Iskander missile demonstrates Russia's willingness to target humanitarian infrastructure, while the reported liberation of two settlements in Sumy region indicates continued Russian ground pressure in the northeast. Ukraine's identification of 500 pre-designated targets in Belarus represents a significant deterrent posture against potential Belarusian escalation.
sections.regional_response: The EU confirmed it will not evacuate diplomats from Kiev despite Russia's warning, signaling continued Western diplomatic presence in the Ukrainian capital. Slovakia's PM Fico stated that many EU countries are ready to join Slovakia's complaint against the European Commission over the ban on Russian gas imports, revealing continued divisions within the EU regarding energy policy toward Russia. Latvia advised residents to plant trees near their homes as protection against Ukrainian kamikaze drones, reflecting growing Baltic concerns about drone incursions. The US confirmed through Secretary Rubio that no negotiations with Ukraine are currently planned, though Washington remains ready to play a constructive role in any future negotiation process.
sections.intelligence_notes: The drone attack pattern shows a sophisticated multi-axis approach with at least 20+ individual UAV groups detected simultaneously from Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Kursk, Bryansk, Black Sea, Kherson, and Belarusian directions, indicating a coordinated and resource-intensive Russian strike operation. The use of reactive (jet-powered) UAVs from multiple northern axes represents an escalation in drone technology that poses greater challenges for Ukrainian air defense due to higher speeds and potentially lower radar signatures. Russian strategic aviation activity with Tu-95MS redeployment, combined with the formal diplomatic warning for foreigners to leave Kyiv, constitutes a credible precursor indicator for potential large-scale cruise missile strikes on the Ukrainian capital in the near term. The Belarusian claim of 116 Ukrainian drone border violations in one week, dismissed by Ukraine's DSP as baseless, may represent Russian-Belarusian information operations to justify potential Belarusian escalation or preemptive military posturing.
